mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates at the zero lower bound suppress risk-free discount rates, compress equity risk premiums, and support higher valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative median 20-day returns over a 2-year rolling window while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9 percent indicates substantial labor market slack; month-over-month declines in UNRATE signal accelerating economic recovery and improving corporate earnings prospects, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE > 9% and declining over 3 months coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production indicates broad-based demand recovery that outpaces supply constraints, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; the combined signal is stronger than either variable in isolation.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce positive median 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is below 0.5 percent, rising consumer price inflation signals successful reflating of nominal demand and erodes real debt burdens, which boosts corporate profitability expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this mechanism is absent under normalized rates.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL rising month-over-month while FEDFUNDS < 0.5% coincides with negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, which translates into positive operating leverage for industrial firms and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 550 thousand units signal a deeply depressed construction cycle; subsequent increases in HOUST indicate a cyclical bottoming in residential investment and broader demand stabilization, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST below 550 and rising over 2 months fails to produce positive median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or signal frequency falls below 5% of months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining unemployment and a federal funds rate below 1 percent activates a credit-fueled wealth transmission channel that raises equity valuations; neither condition in isolation produces the same magnitude of positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint condition of declining UNRATE and FEDFUNDS < 1% produces negative median 20-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP confirms the persistence of aggregate demand expansion beyond high-frequency monthly noise, reduces estimated recession probability, and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth coincides with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of released prints over a 5-year window.
